intro: Stenosolen vanheurckii, also known as Kacip Fatimah, is a flowering plant species native to Malaysia and Southeast Asia, commonly used in traditional medicine. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stenosolen vanheurckii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stenosolen vanheurckii is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or capsule, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions.
